h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Tail's drawn here though — not down at the wheels. Down arrow: Earth pulling. Up arrow: track pushing. Cards sit right. The foot of the arrow...
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was heading toward naming the correct contact object for the up arrow and resolving the card placement as a simple object-label task.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Track on cart — up arrow. [taps blue chip beside arrow tail] Foot here. [taps washer at wheels] Touch there. Different spots. [glances at down arrow] Earth on cart — foot on the chip too. But Earth doesn't touch... no washer for that one.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner uses the new two-marker representation to distinguish contact point from force receiver and transfers it to gravity.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The touch card settled who pushes. It did not settle why the foot sits here.

[places a washer at the wheels and a blue chip on the cart body, beside the arrow tail]

Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force.

Track card to the washer. Up-arrow name to the blue chip: force on cart. Then say the up arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly marks the failed route and introduces a new public representation that splits contact place from force receiver.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force.

Track card to the washer. Up-arrow name to the blue chip: force on cart. Then say the up arrow.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precise, economical, visibly different from object-naming alone, and gives the learner an actionable distinction to apply.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn.
